what_it_does: Renders a static design-system preview card for border-radius tiers, showing five rounded rectangle chips and one pill sample with labels and pixel values.
observations: Includes a dsCard metadata comment with group "Brand", name "Radii", subtitle "Six tiers · 4 → pill", and viewport "700x220".
Links the shared "_card.css" stylesheet and defines file-local CSS for the card, lead text, row layout, swatch stack, chip, pill, label, and pixel text.
Lead copy states usage guidance: chips and checkboxes use 4-6 px, buttons and inputs use 8 px, cards use 12 px, popups use 16 px, and only status dots plus the Chats button use full pill.
Shows six tiers: xs 4 px, sm 6 px, md ★ 8 px, lg 12 px, xl 16 px, and pill 999.
Uses accent gold variables for the swatch backgrounds and borders, plus font and foreground variables for text.
standouts: The file combines visual samples with component-specific radius guidance in the lead copy.
The md tier is marked with a star in its visible label.
The pill tier is represented by a different pill-shaped sample instead of the same chip box.
conventions: Uses a compact standalone HTML preview-card structure with embedded CSS and shared design tokens from CSS variables.
Uses inline styles for the individual chip border-radius values.
Uses short semantic tier names paired with numeric radius labels.
Uses bold inline emphasis inside the lead paragraph for key numeric values.
concerns: The star next to "md" is not explained within the file.
The pill label displays "999" while the CSS uses "999px", unlike the other labels that include "px".
Per-tier radius values are hard-coded inline rather than expressed through named CSS classes or variables in this file.
notes: A compact visual reference for the design system's radius scale and where each tier is intended to be used.
questions: Does the star on "md" indicate the default radius tier?
Is the pill tier label intentionally unitless as "999"?
Are the component usage rules in the lead copy expected to be enforced elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it presents a Radii card with six tiers from 4 px through pill.
intention_signals: It seems intended to make the allowed radius tiers easy to scan visually while tying each tier to concrete interface uses.
novelty: The radius scale includes explicit product guidance for where each radius should appear, including a narrowly scoped full-pill exception.
contribution: Contributes a clear radius taxonomy with visual examples, numeric values, tier names, and usage guidance that a fused system would keep as shape-scale reference data.
summary_for_embedding: Static HTML design-system card visualizing six border-radius tiers from xs 4 px through pill 999 with labels, swatches, and component usage guidance.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for briefing designers or implementers on the intended radius scale and when each rounding tier should be used.
